name: ExtensionMedicationDispense_Category
title: R5: category
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MedicationDispense.category` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MedicationDispense.category` 0..* `CodeableConcept`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MedicationDispense.category` 0..* `CodeableConcept`
*  R4B: `MedicationDispense.category` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`
*  R4: `MedicationDispense.category` 0..1 `CodeableConcept`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MedicationDispense.category` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `MedicationDispense.category` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `MedicationDispense.category` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `MedicationDispense.category` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `MedicationDispense.category` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `MedicationDispense.category` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The category can be used to include where the medication is expected to be consumed or other types of dispenses.  Invariants can be used to bind to different value sets when profiling to bind.

element.definition: Indicates the type of medication dispense (for example, drug classification like ATC, where meds would be administered, legal category of the medication.).
element.comment: The category can be used to include where the medication is expected to be consumed or other types of dispenses.  Invariants can be used to bind to different value sets when profiling to bind.

binding.description: A code describing where the dispensed medication is expected to be consumed or administered.
